body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary); also `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/` **Triggered by:** commit 48e9f7bd852c033dbe6680c68a3197cb55722eca (PR #58274) by @RaviPidaparthi ## What Changed PR #58274 added the following features to the AgentServer packages: 1. **`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`** (Core, `beta.22`) — a new auto-registered ASP.NET Core middleware that logs every inbound HTTP request with method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`, OpenTelemetry trace ID). Successful requests log at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data. 2. **Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation** (Responses, `beta.2`) — SHA-256 hash of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` truncated to 63 lowercase hex chars; falls back to random hex when no conversational context is available. 3. **Handler-level structured logging** (Responses, `beta.2`) — `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) with response ID, conversation ID, and store flag. ## Gaps Found - The **Core README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`) makes no mention of `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. It only says traces come from the Responses/Invocations activity sources, which is now incomplete and misleading — users may not realize all HTTP requests are automatically logged. - The **Core README Key concepts section** does not list `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` or describe the built-in HTTP-level logging as a default server behavior. - The **Responses README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`) does not mention handler-level structured logs or `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, leaving users unable to discover what log categories/levels to configure. - The **Responses README Key concepts section** does not document `agent_session_id` derivation — developers building conversation-aware handlers need to understand that session IDs are deterministically derived from agent name, version, and partition hint, and what the fallback behavior is.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` — edit In the **Key concepts** section, add a new subsection after `### Health endpoint`: ```markdown ### Request logging `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically and logs every inbound HTTP request to the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` logger category: | Event | Log level | Fields | |-------|-----------|--------| | Request started | `Information` | `Method`, `Path`, `XRequestId`, `ClientRequestId`, `TraceId` | | Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `Method`, `Path`, `StatusCode`, `DurationMs`, `XRequestId`, `ClientRequestId`, `TraceId` | | Request failed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | Same as above | Query strings are intentionally excluded from all log messages to avoid logging sensitive data. To adjust the minimum log level, configure the category in your `appsettings.json`: ```json { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware": "Warning" } } } ``` ``` In the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text: ```markdown ### Logging The library registers `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` automatically, which emits structured `Information`-level log messages for every inbound HTTP request and `Warning`-level messages for 4xx/5xx responses. Correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) and the OpenTelemetry trace ID are included in every log entry for cross-service request tracing. The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose startup issues. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit In the **Key concepts** section, add a new subsection after `### Thread safety`: ```markdown ### Session ID derivation Each response is assigned a deterministic `agent_session_id` derived from a SHA-256 hash of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters. The partition hint is extracted from the `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id` in the request. When no conversational context is available, a random hex value is used instead. The derivation priority is: 1. `agent_session_id` provided explicitly in the request payload 2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable 3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash (when conversation context is available) 4. Random 63-char hex (fallback) ``` In the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits structured `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints. Each log entry includes the `ResponseId` and, where applicable, `ConversationId`, `PreviousResponseId`, `Model`, `Streaming`, `Background`, `Store`, `Status`, and `OutputCount` fields: | Endpoint | Log details | |----------|-------------| | POST `/responses` | response ID, model, streaming, background, store, conversation ID, previous response ID | | POST `/responses` (completed, non-streaming) | final status and output count | | GET `/responses/{id}` | retrieved status and output count | | POST `/responses/{id}/cancel` | result status | | DELETE `/responses/{id}` | success flag | | GET `/responses/{id}/input_items` | response ID | The `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call at `Information` level with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers. OpenTelemetry traces are emitted via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose storage and handler issues. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are required for these documentation-only additions. All content is conceptual description and configuration examples that do not need snippet extraction. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Both READMEs already contain all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. ✅ Key concepts — **needs new subsections added** as described in Step 1 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting — **Logging subsections need updating** as described in Step 1 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 6. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` No snippet update or API export is needed since these are documentation-only changes.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [AgentServer] Docs: Document InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware and new Responses logging features
